title: CI matrix (macOS + Linux): fmt, clippy -D warnings, test
description: GitHub/GitLab CI building on macOS + Linux with rustfmt check, clippy denying warnings, and the test suite. Cache the cargo/arti build.

title: Cross-cutting: CI, lint & test gates
description: Build/quality gates parallel to all milestones.

title: Transport lifecycle: warm bootstrap, keepalive, graceful hangup
description: Bootstrap arti in background at launch, cache dir state for fast warm starts, keep circuit warm with PING/PONG, graceful HANGUP teardown. The perf 'hide setup cost' lever.
notes: Partial: warm bootstrap + dir-cache + dial/host live in ArtiTransport. Keepalive PING/PONG handled in app::run_call; explicit graceful HANGUP done. Reconnect + circuit-warm tuning remain.
close_reason: Done (re-close): warm bootstrap + dir cache + keepalive + graceful HANGUP + identity persistence. Auto-reconnect = M2.

title: crypto: HKDF per-call key + AES-256-GCM seal/open + replay window
description: Implement ADR-0004: AeadSuite (AES-256-GCM, ChaCha20-Poly1305 fallback), HKDF-SHA256 call key from PSK + both HELLO nonces, per-direction u64 counter nonces, seq bound into AAD, forward replay window. PSK load/gen.
close_reason: Done + tested: AES-256-GCM/ChaCha20, HKDF per-call key, per-direction counter nonces, seq-in-AAD, replay window. Bug fixed: handshake nonce salt ordering (key agreement).

title: M1: Vertical slice — core encrypted PTT call over Tor
description: A real, snappy, encrypted half-duplex PTT call between two instances over Tor. Exit: two instances hold a clear two-way call; release-to-hear is Tor-bound; cipher mismatch surfaced not silent. See docs/ROADMAP.md.

title: ADR-0001: Rewrite v1 bash to single self-contained Rust binary
description: Accepted. See docs/adr/0001-rewrite-to-single-rust-binary.md. Rust chosen for arti, memory-safe crypto, hw AEAD, cross-platform audio, single artifact.
close_reason: Accepted — recorded in docs/adr/

title: Minimal TUI: host/dial menu, raw-mode PTT, call screen
description: Raw-mode terminal PTT (configurable key, no root); host/listen + dial flows; call screen showing remote .onion, AEAD suite match indicator, hop/anonymity mode, basic ms stats, remote PTT state.
close_reason: Done+verified (re-close): crossterm TUI drives call loop + call-screen header.

title: audio: cpal capture + resample to Opus rate (PTT-gated)
description: Capture mono input via cpal across backends; resample to the Opus rate; produce frames only while PTT is held. Bridge the audio thread to async via a bounded channel.
notes:
Compiles only. AudioEngine::start/capture/stop_capture in audio/mod.rs are todo!() ->  PANICS at runtime until implemented. cpal capture path written but device I/O unverifiable headlessly (no mic in CI). THIS is the critical next step for a runnable binary.
PRIORITY: bump — this + tp-d3z block the binary from running end-to-end.
BLOCKER for runnable binary: AudioEngine start/capture/stop_capture in audio/mod.rs are still todo!(), so terminalphone host/dial panics at runtime until implemented. cpal capture path written but device I/O not verifiable headlessly.
close_reason: Done+verified (re-close after merge revert): cpal capture + synthetic backend; PTT-gated; headless tests green.

title: ADR-0006: Beads for issue & decision tracking
description: Accepted. See docs/adr/0006. Prefix tp-. Work + decisions in one dependency graph.
close_reason: Accepted — recorded in docs/adr/

title: proto: wire frame codec + HELLO handshake
description: Implement SPEC 5.3 framing (ver|type|len|payload) over Conn; typed Frame enum; HELLO exchange (onion addr, AEAD suite id, opus params, 32B nonce); suite negotiation with explicit mismatch abort. All post-HELLO frames sealed.
close_reason: proto done+tested; cargo test green (codec round-trip, handshake key-agreement). Graph blocker was coarse milestone ordering.

title: ADR-0002: Embed Tor in-process via arti
description: Accepted + validated by M0 spike. See docs/adr/0002. launch_onion_service/connect replace torrc+socat. Transport trait keeps system-tor fallback one impl away.
close_reason: Accepted — recorded in docs/adr/

title: ADR-0007: Audio stack cpal + audiopus (libopus)
description: Accepted. See docs/adr/0007. In-process capture/playback (cpal) + Opus (audiopus). 16kHz wideband mono ~24kbps default.
close_reason: Accepted — recorded in docs/adr/

title: audio: audiopus Opus encode/decode
description: Encode captured PCM and decode received frames via audiopus (libopus). Config: 16kHz wideband mono ~24kbps VBR, 20-40ms frames (configurable to 8kHz).
close_reason: audio codec done+tested; audiopus round-trip test green.

title: audio: cpal playback + jitter buffer
description: Play decoded PCM via cpal with a small tunable jitter buffer (ADR-0003): start playout after a configurable lead, then stream smoothly. No clipping on utterance boundaries.
notes: Compiles only. AudioEngine::play is todo!(); JitterBuffer logic implemented + unit-tested (bug fixed: MIN_CAP floor for tiny lead). cpal output device path unverifiable headlessly. Pairs with tp-8fg to make the binary runnable.
close_reason: Done+verified (re-close): cpal playback + jitter buffer; headless tests green.

title: ArtiTransport: host + dial + persisted onion identity
description: Implement Transport via arti (promote the M0 spike): host an onion service from a persisted identity key, dial peers, expose Conn (DataStream). Persist identity under data-dir (0600).
notes: Code complete + compiles: ArtiTransport host/dial promoted from rs-spike behind the Transport trait (arti 0.42, ring provider, display_unredacted, per-role data dirs). Real-Tor e2e NOT verified headlessly — covered by tp-2n2. Single-hop-service left gated (TODO).
close_reason: Done (re-close): ArtiTransport host/dial/identity verified vs arti 0.42. Live two-machine call = tp-hlv (runbook).

title: E2E: two-instance encrypted PTT call over Tor (harness)
description: Promote the M0 spike into an end-to-end test/demo: two instances complete a two-way encrypted PTT call over real Tor. Capture release-to-hear timing.

title: Project skeleton: crate layout, modules, Transport trait, error types
description: Stand up the binary crate per docs/ARCHITECTURE.md: modules (transport/proto/crypto/audio/tui/config/error), the Transport trait, Error enum (thiserror), tokio runtime + rustls ring provider init, config/data-dir resolution. Promote rs-spike into this structure.
close_reason: Crate skeleton done: cargo workspace shares arti build with rs-spike; all modules, Transport trait, Error/Config; cargo build green.

title: ADR-0004: AEAD AES-256-GCM + per-call HKDF key
description: Accepted. See docs/adr/0004. Replaces AES-CBC + per-msg PBKDF2 + optional HMAC. Key derived once/call from PSK+nonces; per-direction counter nonces; seq in AAD.
close_reason: Accepted — recorded in docs/adr/

title: ADR-0005: Speed-first hop default, gated single-hop service
description: Accepted. See docs/adr/0005. Reduced hops by default + max-anonymity toggle; IP-revealing single-hop SERVICE mode is explicit opt-in behind a warning.
close_reason: Accepted — recorded in docs/adr/

title: Pipelined PTT loop (the core call path)
description: Wire it together: while PTT held, capture->encode->seal->send AUDIO frames as produced; on receive, open->decode->enqueue->play. End-of-utterance flush. This realizes the SPEC 3 latency contract.
close_reason: Done+verified (re-close): pipelined run_call proven by selftest (audio+text exact round-trip).

title: ADR-0003: Pipelined store-and-forward audio
description: Accepted. See docs/adr/0003. Half-duplex PTT, transmit overlaps speech, receiver jitter-buffers. Not full-duplex (v1 proved unusable over Tor).
close_reason: Accepted — recorded in docs/adr/

title: config: real TOML parsing (serde) for Config load/save
description: M1 config.rs uses data-dir-rooted defaults; Config::save writes a minimal snapshot. RUNBOOK references config.toml for SpeedMode — implement real serde/toml load so config.toml fields actually parse. No serde dep in M1 by design.
close_reason: Closed

title: In-call encrypted text (MSG frames)
description: Sealed UTF-8 text messages during a call (SPEC 5.3 type 0x03), with TUI compose + display.

title: Fuzz proto codec + replay/nonce hardening
description: cargo-fuzz the frame codec and handshake parser; harden replay window and nonce counters against edge cases.

title: Port remaining v1.1.6 surface
description: PTT chime, circuit-hop display, country exclusion (ExcludeNodes), mid-call cipher/opus change, auto-listen, message stats — onto the v2 core.

title: Secret-at-rest: Argon2id passphrase wrap + plaintext migration
description: Optionally wrap the PSK with an Argon2id-derived key (AES-256-GCM); detect plaintext secrets and offer migration. Stronger than v1's PBKDF2-100k.

title: Hop/speed modes + gated single-hop-service warning
description: Implement ADR-0005: client-side reduced-hop default, max-anonymity toggle, and the explicit-opt-in + warning for IP-revealing single-hop service mode. Surface active mode on the call screen.

title: M3: Feature parity with v1.1.6
description: Port the v1 surface onto the v2 core. Exit: every advertised v1.1.6 feature has a v2 equivalent or documented drop.

title: Integration: in-process loopback Transport mock
description: A Transport impl that connects two instances in-process (no Tor) so the full audio->proto->crypto->proto->audio path is testable in CI fast and deterministically.
close_reason: loopback transport done+tested; backs loopback_call integration test (2/2).

title: Caller ID: .onion exchange + display
description: Exchange and display both parties' .onion on connect (v1 parity).
close_reason: Done+verified (re-close): .onion exchanged in HELLO; TUI shows local+remote onion.

title: M2: Security & robustness hardening
description: Secret-at-rest, replay/nonce hardening, hop modes, reconnect, in-call text. Exit: clean external review of crypto/proto; stable soak.

title: Reproducible builds + signed release artifacts
description: Make the binary as auditable as the script: reproducible build recipe, signed release artifacts, prominent source — the ADR-0001 trust mitigation.

title: CLI flags: --speed / --data-dir / --log (currently config-only)
description: M1 CLI is host | dial <onion> | selftest | --help. SpeedMode is config.toml-driven and data dir is config::default_data_dir(). Expose as CLI flags for operability. Nicety, not M1-blocking.

title: Replace MSG seq-prefix shim with a real Frame::Msg seq field
description: app.rs prefixes an 8-byte big-endian seq onto Frame::Msg.sealed (with_seq_prefix/split_seq_prefix) so MSG shares AUDIO's replay window, because the frozen Frame::Msg wire shape has no seq field. If a future ADR adds a real seq field to Frame::Msg, remove the shim.

title: Voice changer (in-process DSP)
description: Port v1's sox-based voice effects (deep/high/robot/echo/whisper + custom) as in-process DSP before Opus encode.

title: Snowflake / pluggable-transport bridging
description: Censorship-resistance: pluggable transports / Snowflake via arti where supported.

title: Group call UI: live caller count, join/leave
description: Client-side group UX: detect relay mode, show live caller count, handle join/leave without dropping others.

title: Android/Termux audio path
description: Make capture/playback work under Termux (cpal/AAudio vs Termux:API bridge fallback).

title: M4: Group relay
description: Zero-knowledge N-caller relay. Exit: 3-5 caller group call; relay never sees plaintext.

title: M5: Platform reach
description: Android/Termux audio, bridges, reproducible+signed builds. Exit: Termux build talks to desktop build; reproducible-build doc published.

title: Zero-knowledge N-caller relay
description: Relay that forwards sealed AUDIO/MSG/PING between N callers and filters control signals; never holds the PSK; in-memory only.

title: QR identity sharing
description: Render the local .onion as a terminal QR (v1 parity); strip auto-prepended http:// when dialing scanned codes.
